name: Amazon API Gateway
summary: Production monitoring, observability, and security adoption pack for API Gateway REST, HTTP, and WebSocket APIs.

trusted_ai_pillars.name: Observability
trusted_ai_pillars.outcome: Track traffic, client errors, integration errors, latency, throttles, and stage health.

trusted_ai_pillars.name: Explainability
trusted_ai_pillars.outcome: Correlate request IDs, access logs, execution logs, and traces to explain API behavior.

trusted_ai_pillars.name: Quality
trusted_ai_pillars.outcome: Measure availability, latency SLOs, error rate, and integration reliability.

trusted_ai_pillars.name: Ethics and Safety
trusted_ai_pillars.outcome: Protect APIs with authentication, authorization, throttling, WAF, and audit trails.

trusted_ai_pillars.name: Continuous Validation
trusted_ai_pillars.outcome: Continuously validate stage settings, logging, authorization, throttling, and deployment changes.

sections.name: API Traffic
sections.purpose: Request count, stage traffic, method distribution, and client adoption patterns.

sections.name: Errors
sections.purpose: 4xx errors, 5xx errors, integration errors, and authorization failures.

sections.name: Latency
sections.purpose: Overall latency, integration latency, p95/p99 trends, and timeout pressure.

sections.name: Throttling and Quotas
sections.purpose: Throttle events, usage plan pressure, and rate-limit behavior.

sections.name: Access Logs and Traces
sections.purpose: Request IDs, caller identity, route, status, integration status, and X-Ray traces.

sections.name: Security Posture
sections.purpose: WAF findings, auth configuration, resource policy posture, and CloudTrail changes.

metrics.name: Count
metrics.why: Measure request volume and traffic pattern.

metrics.name: 4XXError
metrics.why: Detect client, authorization, or request-shape issues.

metrics.name: IntegrationLatency
metrics.why: Separate backend latency from gateway overhead.

metrics.name: CacheHitCount
metrics.why: Measure cache effectiveness when API caching is enabled.

log_queries.name: API Gateway 5xx requests
log_queries.query: fields @timestamp, requestId, httpMethod, resourcePath, status, integrationStatus, integrationLatency | filter status >= 500 | sort @timestamp desc | limit 50

log_queries.name: API Gateway high latency requests
log_queries.query: fields @timestamp, requestId, httpMethod, resourcePath, status, responseLatency, integrationLatency | filter responseLatency > 1000 | sort responseLatency desc | limit 50

log_queries.name: API Gateway auth failures
log_queries.intent: Find unauthorized or forbidden API requests.
log_queries.query: fields @timestamp, requestId, identity.sourceIp, httpMethod, resourcePath, status | filter status in [401, 403] | sort @timestamp desc | limit 50

alarms.name: APIGateway5xxHigh
alarms.action: Inspect integration health, Lambda/backend logs, and recent deployments.

alarms.name: APIGatewayLatencyHigh
alarms.action: Separate gateway latency from integration latency and inspect backend traces.

alarms.name: APIGateway4xxSpike
alarms.action: Review auth, client changes, request validation, and WAF activity.

alarms.name: APIGatewayThrottleSpike
alarms.action: Review usage plans, client behavior, and rate-limit settings.

security_controls.name: Authentication and authorization
security_controls.evidence: API routes require IAM, JWT, Lambda authorizer, or Cognito authorization where needed.

security_controls.name: WAF protection
security_controls.evidence: Public APIs are associated with WAF for common abuse and injection patterns.

security_controls.name: Access logging
security_controls.evidence: Stage access logs include request ID, caller, route, status, and latency.

security_controls.name: Throttling controls
security_controls.evidence: Stage, route, and usage plan throttling are configured for public-facing APIs.

security_controls.name: CloudTrail auditability
security_controls.evidence: API deployments, stage changes, resource policy changes, and authorizer changes are auditable.

adoption_plan: Enable access logs for all production stages.
Enable X-Ray tracing for critical APIs where supported.
Create dashboards for traffic, errors, latency, throttles, and security posture.
Create alarms for 5xx, latency, 4xx spikes, and throttling.
Review authorization, resource policy, WAF, and throttling controls.
Document API runbooks with request ID correlation.
Promote the pack as the default API Gateway monitoring standard.
